text: The tip of the scapula, this is the posterior axillary line, the anterior axillary line and the mid axillary line.
A line is drawn from the tip of the scapula along the ribs on the fifth intercostal space and
The incision is performed just below the mid axillary line around 3 cm in size.
It's important to keep the incision as dry as possible and take extra care in the hemostasis at this stage because a butt support that is dripping blood can make the
whole operation longer and another important tip is to enter this cavity plenty with the finger simply teasing and dividing the muscle layers and a second round of hemostasis is always useful
incision is measured and it's about three centimeters and now soft tissue retractor inserted is intentionally larger than the incision
This has the very important effect to spread the space up to five centimeters and also allows a better angulation and wider angle of movement for the instruments.
